Notice Of Motion And Motion For Request For Dismissal Of Class And Paga Claims Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Vedang J. Patel In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Prior to the hearing Mr. Patel appears via Zoom. At the instruction of the Court, the clerk informs counsel that Counsel is correct. There is a clerical error on the tentative ruling. The tentative ruling on Line 2, CGC-22-601210 and Line 3, CGC-22-602076 should match. Mr. Patel to submit proposed orders to that effect and asks if Counsel is still contesting the tenative ruling. Counsel states he is not contesting the tentative ruling.

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Antonio Johnson's unopposed Motion for Request [sic] For Dismissal of Class and PAGA Claims Without Prejudice is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Continued To Jun-16-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Request For Dismissal Of Paga Claims Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Vedang J. Patel In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-12-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances. The Coourt adopts its teantaive ruling.

PLAINTIFF ANTONIO JOHNSON's Motion For Request For Dismissal Of PAGA Claims Without
Prejudice.

Plaintiff Antonio Johnson's Motion For Request For Dismissal Of PAGA Claims Without Prejudice is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff has not demonstrated timely filing. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Plaintiff filed this motion on 3/2/2026, which is not 16 court days before 3/12/2026, the hearing date noticed. The notice issues are heightened where, as here, absent individuals' interests are implicated. Further, the timing requirements apply to all motions on the court's motion calendar.

If the court understands this motion, it appears Plaintiff understands his PAGA claim was resolved by way of the settlement in Lewis v. Bayview Hunters Point Foundation for Community Improvement, CGC-23-604338 ("Lewis Action"). If that's true, she must explain why now the claim should be dismissed "without prejudice." If this understanding is not correct, Plaintiff needs to explain. If the effect of an order granting Plaintiff's request is dismissal with prejudice due to the Lewis Action settlement, Plaintiff must explain specifically how such dismissal satisfies California Rules of Court, rule 3.770 and Labor Code 2699. The instant explanation is inadequate.

If Plaintiff's counsel recovered money in connection with the Lewis Settlement or any agreement related to the Lewis settlement, that recovery must be disclosed, explained and justified under rule 3.770. The requested dismissal is related to a settlement and the court will require notice to LWDA.

If Plaintiff's counsel again seeks dismissal of this action and CGC-22-601210, they should timely file separate motions noticed for the same day and include with each a single, joint brief explaining how the requested dismissals are consistent with both rule 3.770 and Labor Code section 2699. In the brief, counsel must justify their filing of two separate actions (and any other actions related to the issues in these cases); counsel should be mindful that they must demonstrate a legitimate purpose not inconsistent with judicial economy and defeat any inference of forum shopping.

The court notes Plaintiff failed to provide a courtesy copy of his papers. Failure to provide courtesy copies is an independent grounds for taking a request or motion off calendar.

If Plaintiff files a subsequent motion seeking the same or substantially similar relief, this Order must be attached and these issues must be addressed.

Prior to the hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)
